lemma_statement: ∃! G : Fin p → PowerSeries R, F = ∑ i, (1+X)^i * phiSeries p (G i) — over general CommRing R
b2_reason: false over R = ℚ: (1+X)^p − 1 = unit·X so phiSeries is surjective (PowerSeries.substInvOfIsUnit applies since coeff 1 = p is a unit) and digits are non-unique; the statement is the p-adically-integral one. Fix: restate over integerRing K, junk-totalise psiSeries via dite. Recorded 2026-06-11 (replan R6 W6b-b1').
counterexample: R = ℚ, p = 2: X has an S-expansion since S = 2X + X^2 has invertible linear coefficient, breaking uniqueness

lemma_statement: seriesEval (phiSeries p G) z = seriesEval G ((1+z)^p - 1) under Summable (RHS-family)
b2_reason: hypothesis too weak: at z = xi-1 the RHS-family summability is trivial while the LHS junk-totalises to 0 for fast-growing G (v_p(coeff k S^n) >= n - k/p bound)
counterexample: p >= 3, z = xi - 1 primitive p-th root, coeff n G of norm p^{2n}: LHS = 0 (divergent tsum junk), RHS = constantCoeff G

lemma_statement: HasSum (fun n : ℕ => z ^ (n + 1) / (n + 1)) (-Complex.log (1 - z)) for ‖z‖ = 1, z ≠ 1
b2_reason: false as a HasSum: ‖z^(n+1)/(n+1)‖ = 1/(n+1) and Σ 1/(n+1) diverges (not_summable_one_div_natCast), so the summand is NOT Summable over ℂ (summable_norm_iff in finite-dim NormedSpace ℝ ℂ), hence HasSum _ L is false for every L. The series is only conditionally convergent on the circle minus {1}. Fix: restate as Tendsto of partial sums over Finset.range, Tendsto (fun N => ∑ n ∈ range N, z^(n+1)/(n+1)) atTop (𝓝 (-log (1-z))) — the honest Abel/Dirichlet-test boundary value. Recorded 2026-06-11 (T610 execution, statement aligned).
counterexample: z = -1 on the unit circle: Σ (-1)^(n+1)/(n+1) = log 2 converges conditionally but ‖summand‖ = 1/(n+1) is not summable, so HasSum is false.

lemma_statement: p·𝓐_ρ(0) = G⁻¹·(p·F̃(0) − Σ_{i<p} F̃(ξ^i−1)) — the c₀-design mass identity (RJW Lem 6.3, distribution-free)
b2_reason: frozen skeleton omitted a norm hypothesis. The proof needs (i) IsUnit(ε^c−1) for one_add_mul_derivative_Ftilde and (ii) a linear coefficient bound ‖coeff n F̃‖ ≤ C·(n+1) (Lem 6.2 ℛ⁺-membership) so the evaluations seriesEval F̃ (ξ^i−1) converge. Both follow from ‖ε^c−1‖ = 1 for the contributing units c (tame D>1). Fix: add hnorm : ∀ c ∈ range N, ¬N∣c → ‖ε^c − 1‖ = 1. Discharged identically in LpFunction_one. Statement otherwise unchanged; PROVEN sorry-free + axiom-clean.
counterexample: none — additive hypothesis, not a falsity; the bare statement is unprovable as stated (F̃-evaluations need a convergence input).

lemma_statement: Σ_{i<p} F̃(ξ^i−1) = θ(p)·F̃(0) — the evaluated trace (RJW TeX 2115–2155)
b2_reason: (a) STATEMENT-FIX: frozen hdom : ∀ c, ¬N∣c → ExtLogDomain(ε^c−1) is too weak; the per-term identity needs the shifted args ξ^i·ε^c−1 in the domain, which fails for hdom in the ramified case (ε^c reducing to 1). Replaced by hnorm : ∀ c, ¬N∣c → ‖ε^c−1‖ = 1 (shifted domains follow by ultrametric isoceles + IsIntegral.of_pow on roots of unity + extLogDomain_of_integral_norm_one). LpFunction_one discharges hnorm from the D>1 cyclotomic-product fact. (b) REMAINING ANALYTIC GAP: the per-term identity seriesEval(logSeriesAt u) z = extLog((1+z)u−1) reduces to extLog(1+w) = padicLog(1+w) for ‖w‖<1, i.e. boundary p-adic-log multiplicativity padicLog(x^n)=n•padicLog x on the FULL open ball ‖x−1‖<1. The arguments ξ^i−1 (i≠0) sit on the exp-ball boundary ‖·‖^{p−1}=p⁻¹ where padicLog_pow/extLog_eq_padicLog (proven only INSIDE the exp ball) do not apply. The ‖·‖<1 log theory (Washington §5.1 / formal-series log additivity evaluated) is not yet in PadicExp — single outstanding prerequisite. Collapse (extLog_prod + IsPrimitiveRoot.prod_pow_sub_one_eq_order), c↦pc bookkeeping, and summability (summable_seriesEval_Ftilde) are in place. Left as sorry pending the boundary-log lemma.
counterexample: none for the corrected (hnorm) statement; the hdom→hnorm fix removes an under-specification, and the analytic gap is a missing lemma not a falsity.

lemma_statement: hnorm hypothesis of the F̃-convergence/trace layer (T615/T616)
b2_reason: STATEMENT-FIX (coprime-guard refinement of the T615/T616 hnorm). The earlier hnorm : ∀ c ∈ range N, ¬N∣c → ‖ε^c−1‖ = 1 is FALSE for N = D·p^n with n≥1: for c = D·j (p^n ∤ j) we have ¬N∣c yet ε^{Dj} = (ε^D)^j is a nontrivial p-power-order root, so ‖ε^{Dj}−1‖ < 1. Hence the ¬N∣c-guarded form is UNDISCHARGEABLE in LpFunction_one. Weakened to the coprime guard hnorm : ∀ c ∈ range N, IsUnit (c : ZMod N) → ‖ε^c−1‖ = 1. Only the unit-c terms contribute to F̃ (θ⁻¹(c) = 0 on non-units by MulChar.map_nonunit), and for unit c (coprime to N) the tame part D>1 gives ‖ε^c−1‖ = 1 (norm_one_sub_pow_eq_one after p-stripping). Proofs of the three lemmas adjusted: by_cases hcd:N∣c → by_cases hcu:IsUnit(c:ZMod N) with branches swapped; the IsUnit(ε^c−1) side-condition of one_add_mul_derivative_Ftilde proved directly (ε^c≠1 for ¬N∣c via IsPrimitiveRoot.pow_eq_one_iff_dvd, field nonzero is a unit). All three remain sorry-free + axiom-clean.
counterexample: N = D·p (n=1), c = D: ¬N∣c but ε^D primitive p-th root, ‖ε^D − 1‖ = p^{-1/(p-1)} < 1, so the old ¬N∣c-guard's hypothesis is unsatisfiable while the conclusion of the guarded statement is vacuous-false at that c.

b2_reason: STATEMENT-FIX (authorised; docstring + this log). Added {εp : integerRing K} (hεp : IsPrimitiveRoot εp (p^n)) (hsplit : ε = (ζ:K)·(εp:K)) to the frozen skeleton. RJW's ε_N is any primitive N-th root; the split form ε = ζ·ε_{p^n} (a primitive tame D-th root times a primitive wild p^n-th root) realises it through the §5 tame/wild data. NECESSITY: the headline Gauss factor G = G(θ⁻¹) = gaussSum θK⁻¹ (zmodChar N hε.pow_eq_one) must split as G = G(η⁻¹)·G(χ⁻¹) via gaussSum_mul_coprime (ValuesAtOneComplex, general domain R = K), whose additive character is zmodChar (D·M) at the PRODUCT root εD·εM; this requires ε to literally be ζ·εp (zmodChar is proof-irrelevant in its pow_eq_one arg, so subst hsplit; rfl bridges the AddChars). The same split feeds the hGtwist closed form (G₀ = K-coercion of the level-p^n Gauss sum, a K-field nonzero NOT an integerRing unit) via the new crt_collapse at the glued root. The displayed equation is otherwise RJW-faithful (D>1 scope = replan R6.4; distribution-free route = replan R6.6). Proven sorry-free + axiom-clean {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}.
counterexample: none — hsplit is a natural instantiation always satisfiable (primitive roots at coprime orders exist over the §5 coefficient ring with enough roots of unity); it strengthens the interface by exposing the split, not by adding a false constraint.

lemma_statement: (1 + X) * derivativeFun (FtildeA p K a) = map (algebraMap...) (Fa p a) for a ≠ 0
b2_reason: false without ¬p∣a: Fa p a = FaNum * Ring.inverse(geomSum) is junk 0 when p∣a (geomSum constant coeff = a not a unit), LHS nonzero

lemma_statement: constantCoeff (FtildeA p K a) = -(extLog p (a:K)) for a ≠ 0
b2_reason: subst junk at a = 0: uA 0 − 1 has constantCoeff −1, HasSubst fails; statement meaningless there
counterexample: a = 0: uA 0 = 0 (0.choose(n+1)=0), constantCoeff(uA 0 − 1) = −1 ≠ 0 so HasSubst (uA 0 − 1) fails

lemma_statement: Module.finrank (K p n) (IntermediateField.extendScalars (K_le_succ p n)) = p
counterexample: n = 0, any p: degree phi(p) = p-1 ≠ p

lemma_statement: theorem norm_cycloUnit_sub_one_lt_one {a : N} (ha : ¬ p ∣ a) {n : N} (hn : 1 ≤ n) : ‖cycloUnit p a n - 1‖ < 1
b2_reason: FALSE as boarded: c_n(a) ≡ a (mod p_n) since c_n(a) − a = Σ_{i<a}(ζ^i−1) has norm < 1; for a ≢ 1 (mod p) the ultrametric isosceles gives ‖c_n(a)−1‖ = ‖a−1‖ = 1. Fixed minimally by adding (ha1 : a ≡ 1 [MOD p]) to this lemma and to cyclo_mem_cycloTower1 / cyclo_mem_unitsTower1 (docstring notes in file). NOT an RJW erratum: TeX 3084 only claims c_n(a) ∈ D_n (unconditional, kept); the C_{∞,1}-membership packaging was the board's own. §12 NOTE: a topological generator of Z_p^x is never ≡ 1 mod p (p>3), so the coleman_to_kl generator's tower is NOT in C_{∞,1}; the §12 lem:closure / fundamental-sequence layer handles the principal-unit normalisation — thread this into the §12 /develop pass.
